transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling TechSupport, this is Mark. How can I help you today?
Customer: Hi Mark, my internet has been cutting out every 15 minutes. It's really frustrating as I'm working from home.
Agent: I'm very sorry to hear that, Sarah. I understand how important a stable connection is for work. Let me check your signal levels from here.
Customer: Okay, thanks.
Agent: I see some fluctuations in your modem upstream Power. This usually points to a loose cable or a faulty splitter. Have you checked the physical connections recently?
Customer: I haven't, actually. Let me look... Oh, the screw-on cable at the back of the modem feels a bit loose.
Agent: Try tightening that, and let me know if the 'Online' light stays steady.
Customer: Okay, it's tight now. The light is solid green.
Agent: Great! I'll monitor it for a few minutes. If it stays stable, we're good. If not, I'll schedule a technician to replace the splitter.
Customer: It seems to be holding up. Thank you for being so patient, Mark.
Agent: You're very welcome, Sarah. Is there anything else I can assist with?
Customer: No, that's all. Have a great day.
Agent: You too, goodbye.

Agent: Hello, this is Sarah from Billing. How can I assist you?
Customer: I'm looking at my bill and there's a $50 charge I don't recognize. I never authorized this.
Agent: Let me pull up your account, Mr. Miller. One moment please... I see the charge here. It's for a premium channel pack added last Tuesday.
Customer: Well, I didn't add it! This is ridiculous.
Agent: I apologize for the confusion. It looks like it might have been added during a promotional call last month. I will remove the charge immediately and issue a credit to your next bill.
Customer: Thank you. I appreciate the quick fix.
Agent: Of course. The credit should appear within 24 hours. Anything else?
Customer: No, that's fine.
